principle: Tamagui is a universal React styling framework with one dep: React
explanation: Tamagui core is a React-only styling framework (~28Kb on web) that supports the full React Native View/Text API, a superset of RN StyleSheet, styled(), hooks, and typed design-system helpers. Runs at runtime and compile-time without the usual zero-runtime library limits. Unstyled and styled versions of all components.

principle: Universal + Compiler solves the frontend trilemma
explanation: Cross-platform React historically forces choose-two-of-three: native feel, native+web deploy, code sharing. Tamagui's optimizing compiler extracts styles, evaluates useTheme/useMedia into CSS variables + media queries, and flattens trees — so you can write once, share ~60-90% code, and still feel native. Gains come from atomic CSS + tree-flattening.

principle: Tamagui 2 minimum versions: React 19, RN 0.81, TS 5
explanation: Tamagui 2 requires React 19+, React Native 0.81+ with the New Architecture enabled (for native apps), and TypeScript 5+. On web there are no version restrictions. These requirements unlock new RN style features like cross-platform boxShadow, filter, backgroundImage, and border shorthand.

principle: Install core for style-only, tamagui for full UI kit
explanation: `yarn add @tamagui/core` gives the styling library alone (~28Kb). `yarn add tamagui` is a superset that adds the full UI kit (Button, Form, Stack, Paragraph, etc.) — anywhere docs say `@tamagui/core` you can swap to `tamagui`. Use `@tamagui/config` for a recommended preset (`defaultConfig` from `@tamagui/config/v5`).

principle: TamaguiProvider wraps your app with config + defaultTheme
explanation: Wrap the root in `<TamaguiProvider config={config} defaultTheme="light">`. Set the root theme via the `defaultTheme` prop rather than an outer `<Theme>` — this enables `fastSchemeChange` on native and lets light/dark use faster media-query updates during SSR on web.

principle: Tamagui config declares tokens, themes, media, animations, shorthands
explanation: `createTamagui({ tokens, themes, media, animations, shorthands, fonts, settings })` returns the typed config passed to TamaguiProvider. Use `declare module '@tamagui/core' { interface TamaguiCustomConfig extends typeof config {} }` so props get strong types. Start from `@tamagui/config/v5` preset for sensible defaults, Tailwind-aligned shorthands, full themes.

principle: tamagui.build.ts is the single source of truth for bundlers
explanation: Central config file read by all bundler plugins (metro, babel, vite, webpack) and the CLI — specifies config path, components to optimize, optional constants whitelist, and extraction options. Avoids duplicating the setup across multiple plugin configs.

principle: Tokens are static variables; themes are dynamic scoped variables
explanation: Tokens = base static values that never change (like global CSS vars). Themes = object maps whose values change contextually in the React tree (like scoped CSS vars). Tokens are fallbacks — when a theme lookup misses, Tamagui checks the matching token. Default categories: size, space, color, radius, zIndex, font, fontSize, lineHeight, letterSpace.

principle: createTokens defines the static design-system primitives
explanation: `createTokens({ size: { sm: 10 }, color: { black: '#000' }, ... })` returns tokens you pass to createTamagui. Access anywhere with `getTokens().size.small` (returns a Variable with `.val` and `.variable`). Use `getTokens({ prefixed: true })['$size'].small` to get prefixed keys. `getVariable` returns CSS var on web, raw value on native.

principle: Token categories map to specific style props automatically
explanation: `size` → width/height/minWidth/minHeight/maxWidth/maxHeight. `zIndex` → zIndex. `radius` → borderRadius and each corner. `color` → color/backgroundColor/borderColor/per-side borderColor. `space` → everything else (padding, margin, gap, top, left, etc.). So `<View padding="$4">` looks up `space.$4`.

principle: Tamagui size scale is hybrid: gradual 1-10, exponential 11-16
explanation: Most systems use pure exponential (2,4,8,16). Tamagui uses a gradual scale 1-10 so $2 is tiny UI, $4 is average, $5-$6 emphasized, $7+ for hero/CTA. Above 10 it turns exponential for chunky sizes. Three sub-1 quantum tokens ($0.25, $0.5, $0.75) exist for every group. All token groups (size, space, radius, font) align to the same 1-16 keys so components compose.

principle: Specific tokens let you add custom categories with dotted access
explanation: Since v1.34 you can add arbitrary token categories like `icon: { small: 16, medium: 24 }`. Access with the category-first syntax: `<Stack width="$icon.small" />`. Use the `px()` helper from @tamagui/core for custom categories to emit `100px` on web and `100` on native; built-in size/space/radius already add px where needed.

principle: Themes are objects of contextual values resolved upwards
explanation: Define themes with any keys: `{ background: '#000', color: '#fff' }`. Style props that use `$key` first look up the theme, then fall back through parent themes, ultimately to tokens. Themes are like scoped CSS variables vs tokens which are global. Use `useTheme()` hook to read values in code.

principle: Sub-themes nest with parent_child naming
explanation: Name themes `parentName_subName` and Tamagui lets them nest: `dark_green`, `light_green_subtle`. Wrap with `<Theme name="dark"><Theme name="green"><Button theme="subtle"/></Theme></Theme>`. Sub-themes can be subsets of parents — missing keys resolve upward. Also pass full names directly: `<Button theme="green_subtle"/>`.

principle: Component themes: name_ComponentName for targeted overrides
explanation: Any `styled(View, { name: 'Circle' })` looks for a theme ending `_Circle`. Define `dark_Circle` or `dark_green_Circle` to change defaults only for Circles. Component-theme names must be capitalized. This lets library authors keep design authority while users customize per-component. Removes the `name` prop from defaultProps at runtime.

principle: tamagui UI kit standardizes theme keys: background, color, borderColor, shadowColor, placeholderColor
explanation: Tamagui UI components read a fixed set of keys: background, color, borderColor, shadowColor, placeholderColor. Each has pseudo variants: backgroundHover, backgroundPress, backgroundFocus; same for color and borderColor. Define these in every theme to re-theme the whole UI kit together. All are optional — set `unstyled` on any component to skip them.

principle: Type themes with `satisfies` and a BaseTheme anchor
explanation: Put themes in a separate file. Define `const light = { ... }`, derive `type BaseTheme = typeof light`, then declare the rest `const dark: BaseTheme = { ... }`. Export all themes with `satisfies { [key: string]: BaseTheme }` so TypeScript catches missing keys while preserving exact literal types. Requires TS 4.9+.

principle: Dynamic themes: addTheme, updateTheme, replaceTheme
explanation: `@tamagui/theme` exports `addTheme`, `updateTheme` (merges values), and `replaceTheme` (replaces whole theme). Dynamic changes apply on the client only — ignored during SSR. Useful for user-controlled palettes or lazy-loaded theme bundles.

principle: Tree-shake themes JS in prod by relying on CSS hydration
explanation: For production client bundles, export an empty themes object: `process.env.TAMAGUI_IS_SERVER ? themesIn : {} as typeof themesIn`. The Next.js plugin sets TAMAGUI_IS_SERVER automatically. Tamagui rehydrates by scanning CSS in the browser (low single-digit ms) and saves significant JS size. Only works when the getCSS output is served on initial load.

principle: createThemeBuilder for programmatic theme suites
explanation: Three progressive helpers: (1) `createV5Theme` — quick defaults with optional palette overrides; (2) `createThemes` — palette-interpolation helpers with base/accent/children levels; (3) `createThemeBuilder` — low-level chainable API for full control over palettes, templates (map prop → palette index), and sub-theme hierarchy. Negative indices count backward. Build at compile time to shrink bundles.
